items.briefing:
Australian businesses, including training organisations, are being urged to rethink their cybersecurity approach as AI becomes a double-edged sword — helping defenders but also giving attackers new tools to bypass security controls. Researchers warn that cybercriminals are now exploiting legitimate AI platforms to carry out attacks, making threats harder to detect. Meanwhile, a ransomware attack on a Japanese frozen-food company serves as a reminder that operational disruptions from cyber incidents can be severe and swift.

Closer to home, Australian universities are increasing AI investment but still have notable security gaps to address. Staff should be cautious about any unexpected app downloads or links, as fake apps disguised as legitimate services are being used globally to spy on users.

**Practical tip:** Only install apps or software from official, trusted sources — never from links shared via email or messaging apps.

featured.title: AI arms race forcing businesses to rethink cybersecurity
featured.quote: AI is forcing Australian businesses to fundamentally rethink their cybersecurity strategies and response times.
